info.name: Global Earthquakes API (Full-Stack 2026)
info.description:
# 🌍 Global Earthquakes Backend System - Comprehensive API Reference

Welcome to the official, enterprise-grade API test suite and documentation for the **Global Earthquakes Backend System**. This API acts as the central data gateway and processing engine for global seismic events, crowd-sourced felt reports, and statistical analytics.

---

## 🚀 Architectural Overview
The backend is built using the high-performance **Node.js, Express, and MongoDB (Mongoose)** stack, organized under the industry-standard **Model-View-Controller (MVC)** architectural pattern. 

### Core Highlights:
1. **Real-time Live Integration**: Integrates directly with the **United States Geological Survey (USGS) GeoJSON Feed** to automatically seed live, high-magnitude seismic events.
2. **Advanced Geo-Spatial Queries**: Employs spherical coordinate algorithms (`$geoWithin` and `$centerSphere`) using 2D sphere spatial indexes to execute geographical proximity searches.
3. **High-Performance Aggregations**: Uses MongoDB's parallel multi-stage `$facet` pipelines to compile statistical analytics, regional average depths, monthly trends, and severity buckets in a single database round-trip.
4. **Role-Based Access Control (RBAC)**: Enforces fine-grained credential verification (JWT-based) to safeguard data mutating endpoints (`POST`, `PUT`, `DELETE`).

---

## ⚙️ Authentication & Environment Variables

This collection is configured to run out-of-the-box in both local and production environments. It utilizes **Collection-level Variables** to maintain state, meaning you do not have to manually copy and paste authentication tokens!

### State-Tracking Variables:
* `baseUrl`: The root API endpoint. Defaults to `http://localhost:5000/api/v1`.
* `userToken`: Set dynamically when you execute the `Login User` request.
* `adminToken`: Set dynamically when you execute the `Login Admin` request.
* `reportId`: Set dynamically when you submit a new Felt Report.

---

## 📋 Table of Contents & Quick Links
* 🔒 [Authentication Services](#folder-Auth) - Manage user registration, secure login sessions, and JWT profile lookups.
* 🌍 [Earthquake Event Channels](#folder-Earthquakes) - Query paginated results, execute geospatial distance searches, compile aggregations, and perform CRUD actions.
* 📊 [Crowd-Sourced Felt Reports](#folder-Felt-Reports) - Log intensity felt reports and query regional feedback.
* 🏥 [System Health Heartbeat](#folder-System) - Monitor CPU usage, memory, database readiness, and server uptime.
* 💻 [Project Presentation Guide](file:///c:/Users/Admin/OneDrive/Desktop/global_earthquakes/PROJECT_PRESENTATION_GUIDE.md) - Your local cheat sheet for the live presentation.
* 🛰️ [Local API Documentation Markdown](file:///c:/Users/Admin/OneDrive/Desktop/global_earthquakes/API_DOCUMENTATION.md) - Complete offline reference guide.

item.name: Auth
item.description:
# 🔒 Authentication & Identity Management

This folder compiles all user registration, secure credential validation, profile retrieval, and session termination endpoints. It uses modern **JWT credentials security** and supports both secure Authorization headers and HttpOnly secure cookies.

### 🛡️ Security Policies:
* Passwords are automatically hashed on the database level using `bcrypt` (10 rounds).
* Authentication tokens expire in `7 days` by default.

request.description:
### 🔹 Endpoint: `POST /auth/register` (Public)

Registers a new standard user account in the system database. Upon successful registration, the API automatically issues a secure JWT token returned in the payload and binds an HttpOnly cookie to establish a session.

#### 📥 Expected JSON Input:
* `username` (string, required): Minimum 3 characters. Unique.
* `email` (string, required): Valid email address formatting. Unique.
* `password` (string, required): Minimum 6 characters.

#### 📤 Example Successful Response (201 Created):
```json
{
  "success": true,
  "statusCode": 201,
  "message": "User registered successfully",
  "data": {
    "user": {
      "id": "665c84d6b...",
      "username": "testuser",
      "email": "testuser@earthquakes.com",
      "role": "user"
    },
    "token": "eyJhbGciOiJIUzI1NiIsIn..."
  }
}
```

### 🔹 Endpoint: `POST /auth/register` (Admin Spec, Public)

This request registers a user with explicit `admin` status. In a production system, this route is restricted or protected, but it is available here to easily bootstrap default administrative testing capabilities.

#### 📥 Expected JSON Input:
* `username`: "testadmin"
* `email`: "testadmin@earthquakes.com"
* `password`: "admin123"
* `role`: "admin"

### 🔹 Endpoint: `POST /auth/login` (Public)

Authenticates a standard user. It includes a Postman **Post-response script** that extracts the returned JWT token from the response payload and automatically saves it to your collection variable `{{userToken}}`.

#### 💡 Automation Highlight:
Subsequent requests requiring standard user privileges automatically use the `{{userToken}}` variable in their headers, creating a completely automated, click-and-run testing flow.

### 🔹 Endpoint: `POST /auth/login` (Admin Profile, Public)

Authenticates an administrative user. It includes an automated Post-response script saving the administrative token to the collection variable `{{adminToken}}`.

#### 💡 Automation Highlight:
Subsequent requests requiring administrative permissions (such as manual logging, updating, or deleting seismic events) automatically use the `{{adminToken}}` variable in their Authorization headers.

### 🔹 Endpoint: `GET /auth/profile` (Protected)

Retrieves profile details (username, email, role) of the currently authenticated user. 

* **Required Headers**: `Authorization: Bearer {{userToken}}`
* **Behavior**: Decodes the JWT token payload, verifies database record existence, and strips the hashed password before returning user metadata.

### 🔹 Endpoint: `POST /auth/logout` (Public)

Clears the authenticated secure cookie state from the browser and overrides the session state.

item.name: Earthquakes
item.description:
# 🌍 Earthquake Event Channels & Queries

This module contains all channels for querying, searching, filtering, and managing global seismic events. It showcases advanced MongoDB features such as **dynamic query building**, **2D spherical geospatial calculations**, and **complex aggregation framework pipelines**.

request.description:
### 🔹 Endpoint: `GET /earthquakes` (Public)

Retrieves a list of seismic events, leveraging a **reusable pagination utility** and a **dynamic sort builder**.

#### 📈 Query Parameters:
* `page` (number, optional): Page index to query. Defaults to `1`.
* `limit` (number, optional): Records per page. Defaults to `20`.
* `sort` (string, optional): Sort sorting. E.g. `-time` (newest first), `mag` (weakest first), `-mag` (strongest first).

#### 📥 Example Response Meta Structure:
```json
"meta": {
  "totalRecords": 305,
  "totalPages": 31,
  "currentPage": 1,
  "limit": 10,
  "hasNextPage": true,
  "hasPrevPage": false
}
```

### 🔹 Endpoint: `GET /earthquakes` (Filtered, Public)

Showcases the backend's **dynamic filter builder**. It dynamically parses boundaries for magnitudes and chronological conditions without cluttering database layers.

#### 📈 Filter Parameters:
* `minMag` (number): Filters events where magnitude is `$gte` minMag.
* `maxMag` (number): Filters events where magnitude is `$lte` maxMag.
* `status` (string): Filters reviewed state (`reviewed` or `automatic`).

### 🔹 Endpoint: `GET /earthquakes` (Sub-string search, Public)

Executes a case-insensitive regular expression (`$regex`) sub-string match query on the `place` name attribute (e.g. finding events containing "California", "Fiji", or "Japan").

### 🔹 Endpoint: `GET /earthquakes` (Geospatial, Public)

Performs an advanced coordinates proximity lookup using MongoDB's `$geoWithin` and `$centerSphere` spherical calculations. 

* **Requires**: A `2dsphere` geospatial index on the database `geometry` coordinate key (pre-configured in your database schema).
* **Query Parameters**:
  * `latitude` (number, required): Center latitude coordinate.
  * `longitude` (number, required): Center longitude coordinate.
  * `maxDistanceKm` (number, optional): Distance radius. Defaults to `500`.

### 🔹 Endpoint: `GET /earthquakes/stats` (Public)

#### 📈 Central Statistical Aggregation Engine
This high-performance statistical compiler runs a complex MongoDB Aggregation Pipeline processing parallel analysis tracks in **one database round-trip** via `$facet`:

1. **severityTiers**: Groups events into magnitude classifications (`$bucket` boundaries: 0, 4.0, 5.0, 6.0, 7.0, 10.0).
2. **tsunamiRisk**: Calculates risk trigger ratios.
3. **regionalFrequencies**: Groups reporting networks, ranking frequencies, maximum magnitude peaks, and average event depths.
4. **monthlyTrends**: Sorts event rates chronologically over the past 12 months.
5. **topSignificantEvents**: Filters and ranks the top 5 highest impact occurrences based on their significance index.

### 🔹 Endpoint: `GET /earthquakes/:id` (Public)

Retrieves detail payload records for a single earthquake event.

* **Flexibility**: The backend dynamically detects whether the passed `:id` is a standard 24-character Mongoose MongoDB `ObjectID` or a custom alphanumeric USGS `eventId` string (e.g. `mock100001`), querying the appropriate index dynamically.

### 🔹 Endpoint: `POST /earthquakes` (Admin-Only)

Logs a new manual seismic event in the system.

* **Access Restrictions**: Requires a valid administrative login session token. Enforced via global Role-Based Access Control (`protect` & `authorize('admin')` middleware).
* **Required Headers**: `Authorization: Bearer {{adminToken}}`
* **Request Validation**: Coordinates must be formatted as exactly 3 numbers: `[longitude, latitude, depth]` in accordance with Mongoose schema rules.

### 🔹 Endpoint: `PUT /earthquakes/:id` (Admin-Only)

Updates properties of an existing seismic event log.

* **Required Headers**: `Authorization: Bearer {{adminToken}}`
* **Parameters**: Accepts both MongoDB Object IDs and custom USGS Event ID strings.

### 🔹 Endpoint: `DELETE /earthquakes/:id` (Admin-Only)

Removes an earthquake log permanently from the database. Enforces strict administrative session locks.

item.name: Felt Reports
item.description:
# 📊 Crowd-Sourced Intensity Felt Reports

This directory manages public crowd-sourced felt intensity feedback reports. It allows standard users to log shaking intensities (on a scale of 1-10) and automatically maintains referential document-level metadata counters in the associated parent Earthquake logs.

request.description:
### 🔹 Endpoint: `POST /reports` (Protected)

Logs a crowd felt intensity report for an event. It automatically updates the parent `Earthquake` document's felt counter and contains a script saving the generated ID to `{{reportId}}`.

* **Required Headers**: `Authorization: Bearer {{userToken}}`
* **Unique Conditions Enforced**: An index on `{ user: 1, earthquake: 1 }` prevents a single user from submitting duplicate reports for the same event.
* **Atomic Increments**: Employs Mongoose atomic `$inc` updates on the parent earthquake document, maintaining exact relational counts under heavy concurrencies.

### 🔹 Endpoint: `GET /reports/earthquake/:eventId` (Public)

Retrieves all public crowd-sourced felt reports submitted for an event, automatically populating the associated user parameters (username, email, role) while hiding database credentials.

### 🔹 Endpoint: `DELETE /reports/:id` (Protected)

Deletes a felt report.

* **Access Restrictions**: Enforces strict permission limits. A user can only delete a report if they are the original author of the document, or if they have administrative permissions (`admin` role).
* **Atomic Decrements**: Deleting a report automatically fires a `$inc: -1` operator on the parent earthquake's counters to preserve data integrity.

item.name: System
item.description:
# 🏥 System Health Monitoring

Endpoints monitoring active server diagnostics, uptime load metrics, and database connectivity ready-states.

request.description:
### 🔹 Endpoint: `GET /health` (Public)

Compiles server-side system health metrics.

#### 📈 Diagnostics Returned:
* Server ready status.
* Exact ISO timestamps.
* Server uptime in seconds.
* Comprehensive memory consumption profiles (`rss`, `heapTotal`, `heapUsed`, `external`, `arrayBuffers`).
* Detailed CPU load cycles (`user`, `system`).
* Active MongoDB connection states (0: Disconnected, 1: Connected, 2: Connecting, 3: Disconnecting).
